Multiple sources emphasize its utility, noting its high sensitivity and specificity comparable to invasive methods. Additionally, several studies indicate that the UBT is preferred because it offers a non-invasive and efficient way to verify the success of eradication therapy. While alternatives exist, such as stool antigen tests or cultures, the UBT stands out as the gold-standard tool endorsed by many authoritative bodies and clinical trials.
